acceptance_criteria: Retroactive changes are applied to service records, reports, and invoicing management tools so that service responsibility classifications remain accurate.

Users with sufficient access rights can define a start date for when the change to service responsibility comes into effect, at which point the system identifies and updates all related uninvoiced events accordingly.
The change to service responsibility is automatically reflected in the linked processes, such as:
Service event logs (e.g. waste collections and container services).
Eligibility for certain services or pricing based on the updated responsibility.
Compliance reporting to distinguish between municipal and market-based service categories.
Retroactive updates are propagated throughout the system, ensuring consistency across modules such as route planning, service management, and dashboards.
Users can generate reports based on either the original or updated service responsibility classifications, providing flexibility for internal and external audits.
The system logs all retroactive changes and stores details such as the user who made the change, timestamps, and the events affected by the change.
Role-based access rights ensure that only authorised personnel can make retroactive changes, improving both data security and regulatory compliance.
The system limits retroactive changes to events that have not yet been invoiced. This safeguards the integrity of issued invoices while still allowing administrative corrections to pending data.
Thanks to retroactive changes in service responsibility, waste companies can correct administrative data while ensuring data accuracy and compliance.
The functionality can be applied in various scenarios and supports the needs of waste company administration, compliance officers, and service managers.

A municipality (Customer A) has handled waste services for residential and commercial properties under municipal service responsibility. Mid-year, new regulations transfer responsibility for commercial properties to a regional waste authority effective from 1 January. The user updates the service responsibility for these properties retroactively to that date. The system adjusts the unbilled service data to match the new classification while maintaining accurate reporting for regulatory compliance.

A business customer (Customer B) was mistakenly assigned TSV service responsibility (municipal secondary responsibility). The error is detected after reviewing the service records. An office worker performs a retroactive update and corrects the responsibility from the date the error began. The system updates all pending service events to the correct classification but does not affect previously issued invoices, so that accounting requirements are met.

Customer C’s property is located in a remote area where, following a tariff change, the waste collection service is modified for the summer months. The seasonal change must be accurately reflected in both the customer’s service log and the compliance reports. The office administrator updates the seasonal service retroactively so that it covers only the agreed summer period and ensures that operational schedules and reports match the contractual terms.

requirements.summary: 3.1.18 Alert when euro limit is exceeded according to responsibility classification
acceptance_criteria: 1. Admin users can configure and adjust annual euro billing thresholds per service responsibility classification.
2. The system continuously monitors annual billed amounts for business customers and creates a trigger when the configured threshold is exceeded.
3. Back-office users can filter and search for business customers by service responsibility classification and billing threshold status (exceeded / not exceeded).
4. Users can convert triggers into tickets for structured case management and follow-up.
5. From a trigger or ticket, users can view the customer's details, billing history, and current service responsibility classification.
6. Users can either update the customer's service responsibility to a market-based classification, or document a justification for retaining the current municipal classification.
7. All trigger events, review decisions, and service responsibility changes are recorded in a full audit trail for compliance and reporting purposes.
8. Only authorised personnel (based on assigned roles) can configure billing thresholds, create triggers, and manage tickets.

The service responsibility classification of business customers creates a stimulus and, if necessary, also a ticket, when a certain annual billable euro amount has been exceeded. The euro limit must be adjustable. In the MVP phase, it is enough to create a stimulus that can be connected to automatic workflows that will be specified later in further development. However, the office workers of the waste companies must be able to easily search for problems that have arisen in order to resolve them manually even before the automations are built. Before building the automation, it is necessary to be able to define the generated ticket from the trigger. Waste Act § 33 The municipality's secondary waste management service

1.) The waste company's office worker must apply for business customers who were in the municipal service responsibility classification and who have been invoiced for more than €2,000 within a year. As a result, the service responsibility of these companies must be changed, or in order to keep the service responsibility unchanged, appropriate grounds must be found for the situation. The office worker finds the right companies with the help of the alarms / tickets that have appeared.

2.) The waste company wants to monitor company-specific turnovers in order to carry out credit checks. In this case, the waste company wants the trigger to occur when the turnover exceeds the regulated limit
Billing events must be transferable from one customer to another, for example when a resident changes.
Events related to shared services and waste reception must also be transferable from one customer to another.
A billing event refers to an action to be invoiced from the customer, such as a container emptying or a weighbridge weighing.
1. A customer reports that they have already moved out of the apartment but are still receiving invoices that should no longer have been sent to them.
2. The waste company’s invoicing user notices that new invoicing events are also scheduled for this customer. The user transfers the uninvoiced events to the new resident.
3. The invoiced events are corrected by the user.
Users can make retroactive price changes as follows:
**Selecting a date range:** The system identifies all unbilled events for the specified time period.
**Applying new prices:** Users can change prices based on percentage or fixed euro adjustments. These updates can be linked to products, services, or customer contracts.
**Mass updates or single updates:** Users can flexibly update the prices of a single event or perform batch changes across multiple events at the same time.

The system ensures that retroactive changes do not affect events that have already been invoiced, preserving data integrity and compliance with financial reporting requirements. Once the new prices have been applied, the system recalculates the affected events, updates their pricing information, and prepares them for invoicing according to the corrected prices.

In addition, price adjustments are logged, including the reason for the change, the user who performed the update, and the events affected by the change. This increases transparency and supports auditability for regulatory compliance and internal control.

The solution also includes tools for validating and previewing the impact of retroactive price changes before they are finalised. Users can review the updated pricing information to ensure that the changes have been applied correctly and that they comply with tariff or contractual terms.
**Manual exclusion:** Users can exclude an individual event from invoicing. This allows flexibility to resolve ambiguities or clarify details with the customer before invoicing the event.

**Bulk exclusion:** Office staff can select and exclude multiple events from invoicing in a single step. This is particularly useful when handling large transaction volumes, such as for housing companies or customers delivering bulk waste to reception facilities.

**Selective invoicing:** The system allows users to extract specific components (e.g., base fees) from events and process them separately. This feature is useful in cases where only certain charges need to be invoiced immediately, while others are temporarily left out of billing.

Events marked as excluded remain in the system but are flagged as non-billable. Users can update the status of these events later once the issue has been resolved. In addition, excluded events are tracked in the system with details such as the reason for exclusion, user actions, and timestamps.

The system also supports situations where services or products are not invoiced to the customer but still require contractor compensation. In these cases, the system separates customer billing from contractor payment workflows, ensuring that contractors are paid for completed services even if customer invoicing is delayed or omitted.
Billing events must be viewable and filterable according to the current invoicing process, for example by:
Date
Products of a specific service
Municipal data
Service responsibility
The FINVOICE material is to be generated during the invoicing run.
The FINVOICE specification/definition is to be agreed during implementation.
**The current latest standard is Finvoice 3**

The system generates a dataset that contains all the mandatory FINVOICE fields as well as the information required for accounting as described in the requirement. A single invoice line visible to the customer may be split in the FINVOICE data across different ledger accounts for accounting purposes.

**FINVOICE compatibility:** The solution will generate invoices in the FINVOICE format and transmit them to an external invoicing system along with the required company-specific accounting identifiers such as cost center, service responsibility, and waste type.
The solution will support multiple VAT rates, apply the correct VAT rate, and allow VAT rates with decimals to ensure compliance with tax regulations.
The solution will be integrated with external systems.
Waste management companies can define, during the implementation phase, the required format for entering numbers, and the system will apply that model going forward when validating numbering.

During invoice creation, invoicing users can either define the invoice number series manually, or select the series to be used from pre-defined invoice number series (e.g., sludge invoice, credit invoice, public or private waste collection, annual invoice).
The waste company can define the structure of the invoice number in an invoicing template. The invoice number must always be unique for each invoice.
Waste management companies have full control over defining and managing invoice number series.
The system can prevent duplicate and invalid numbering by applying numbering restrictions.
Authorized users can select an appropriate invoice number series based on pre-defined categories.
When performing invoicing, the user selects the desired invoice template, which has an invoice number series defined for the type of invoicing in question.

requirements.summary: 3.4.4 Invoice data based on language selection
acceptance_criteria: When generating invoice data, the system selects the correct language version based on the language setting saved in the customer's profile.

The selected language is integrated into the external invoicing system in the same way as other criteria and elements defined for the invoice template. The system's default primary language is Finnish.
A language code can be defined for a customer account (Finnish, Swedish, or English).
Products have separate names defined for each language.
Translations are defined for product units.
Addresses can be stored in two languages, and the address is exported to the FINVOICE file according to the selected language.
The system sets the invoice template language according to the customer's selection, and exports this in the FINVOICE file.
The invoice template information can be set for:
A customer
A property/site
A contract
During an invoicing run
The WasteHero system will not create or manage invoice templates but rather send information about the desired invoice template to the invoicing system as a part of the FINVOICE data.

The WasteHero application will send the template setting to an external invoicing system using invoice template codes, which will ensure compatibility between the invoice template information configured in WasteHero and the templates managed in the external invoicing system.

acceptance_criteria: The WasteHero system enables real-time retrieval and display of invoice images stored in external invoicing systems. This functionality ensures that waste management company staff can view invoice details directly within the ERP system, even when the invoices have been generated and stored in an external system. The integration provides a seamless user experience, allowing users to access invoice images without switching between platforms.

When a user selects an invoice in the ERP system, the system sends a request to the external invoicing system to retrieve the corresponding invoice image. The image is then displayed in a dedicated viewing window within WasteHero ERP, eliminating the need to manually download files or open them in a separate system. This process ensures that users can quickly verify invoice details and that all financial information is readily accessible.

The integration is designed to work with multiple external invoicing service providers, ensuring flexibility regardless of where invoices are archived. In addition, role-based access controls determine which users are allowed to view invoice images, ensuring compliance with information security policies. If an invoice image cannot be retrieved, the system provides clear error messages and troubleshooting guidance.

**Accounts receivable is not stored in the ERP system:** WasteHero ERP does not maintain accounts receivable data. Instead, it retrieves invoice images from the external invoicing system and displays them in the ERP for user review.
**Consistent invoice image viewing:** The ERP displays invoice images exclusively via integration; invoices remain stored in the external system.
**Ensured compatibility:** The ERP communicates with external invoicing systems via APIs, enabling smooth retrieval of invoice images without additional manual steps.
**User access control:** Only authorised users can view invoice images, ensuring compliance with financial and security regulations.
**Real-time invoice image retrieval:** Users can select an invoice in the ERP system, triggering an automatic request to fetch the corresponding invoice image from the external system.
**Seamless integration with external systems:** The ERP connects to external invoicing systems via APIs or integration interfaces to ensure smooth image retrieval.
**Invoice display within the ERP:** Retrieved invoice images are displayed in a dedicated viewing window within the WasteHero platform, avoiding manual downloads or system switching.
**Consistent invoice tracking:** Each invoice remains linked to its image, ensuring that financial data, payment details, and invoicing history are easily accessible.
**Error handling and validation:** If an invoice image cannot be retrieved, the system presents appropriate error messages and troubleshooting options to the user.

A waste management company uses an external invoicing system to generate and store customer invoices, while WasteHero ERP manages invoice-related data. An accountant needs to review an invoice issued three months earlier.
1. The accountant logs in to WasteHero ERP and searches for the invoice by its number.
2. The system sends a request to the external invoicing platform to retrieve the corresponding invoice image.
3. The invoice image is displayed in a window within the ERP system, allowing the accountant to review the details without leaving the application.

If the invoice is archived to an external service provider and the sales ledger is in ERP, the image of the invoice is displayed via the integration interface so that the invoice is selected in the system and the image of the invoice opens in a window for the user to see.

The WasteHero ERP system supports attaching PDF documents to invoicing data in accordance with the FINVOICE format guidelines. This ensures that the invoicing data sent via external invoicing systems can include the necessary attachments, such as service agreements, waste reports, or terms and conditions.

Users can upload and link attachment files directly to the invoicing data before transmission. Each attachment follows the FINVOICE _AttachmentDetails_ structure, which defines metadata such as _AttachmentIdentifier_, _AttachmentName_, and _AttachmentMimeType_. The ERP system encodes attachments in Base64 format, ensuring secure data transfer while complying with size limitations (a maximum of 10 attachments, with a total size of less than 1 MB before encoding). Only certain file formats, such as PDF/A, JPEG, and PNG, are permitted, and automatic validation prevents the upload of invalid files.

The system integrates with external invoicing services to enable seamless transmission of both invoicing data and attachments. When invoicing data is generated, the ERP system automatically validates that attachment formats and limitations comply with FINVOICE standards. If errors are detected—such as exceeded size limits or unsupported file types—the system notifies the user before transmission.

To ensure confidentiality, attachments can be classified using SEI codes (e.g. SEI01 for restricted documents), ensuring compliance with security standards. The ERP system also generates a SHA1 checksum for each attachment, maintaining data integrity and verification across systems.
**Invoice vs. invoicing data:** The ERP system produces invoicing data (not the final invoice), including all required attachments, before sending it to the external invoicing system for processing.

**Invoice selection in the ERP:** When the solution description states that an official selects an invoice, this refers to selecting the invoicing data in the ERP system for further processing, validation, and attachment management prior to sending it to the external system.

**Attachment handling in the ERP vs. invoicing system:** Depending on the service provider and the outcome of the procurement process, attachment management may take place either in the ERP system or in the external invoicing system. The ERP system ensures that all required metadata and compliance checks are fulfilled before invoicing data is sent externally.
**PDF attachments in invoicing data:** Users can upload and link attachment files to invoicing data in the ERP system before transmission.
**Integration with external invoicing systems:** The ERP system ensures that all attachments are included and validated in accordance with FINVOICE guidelines before invoicing data is sent.
**Validation and compliance:** Automatic checks ensure that attachments comply with FINVOICE requirements (file types, size limits, completeness of metadata, and checksum validation).
**Security and confidentiality:** SEI codes classify attachments, and SHA1 checksums ensure document integrity.
**Error handling and notifications:** The system notifies users if attachments exceed size limits or use unsupported formats before the invoicing data is sent externally.

A waste management company creates invoicing data for a customer that includes transportation and treatment fees. The invoicing user must attach a customer-specific contract and a waste report as supporting documents.
1. The official selects the invoicing data in the ERP system and uploads two PDF attachments, ensuring they meet FINVOICE PDF/A requirements.
2. The system encodes the files in Base64 format, generates SHA1 checksums, and verifies compliance with size and format restrictions.
3. Once the invoicing data and attachments are validated, they are transmitted to the external invoicing system via the FINVOICE interface.
4. The customer views the invoice in their online banking portal, where the attachments are available for download as additional pages.

If necessary, a pdf attachment must be attached to the individual invoice. FINVOICE INSTRUCTIONS: ([https://file.finanssiala.fi/finvoice/Finvoice_3_0_soveltamishohje.pdf](https://file.finanssiala.fi/finvoice/Finvoice_3_0_soveltamishohje.pdf)) The details of individual attachments are given in the AttachmentDetails section. There can be several attachments, in which case the AttachmentDetails part is repeated according to the number of attachments. There may be no more than ten attachments and their total size may not exceed one megabyte before encoding. The restrictions on the number and size of attachments are checked programmatically at reception. The software that generates and/or sends invoices must ensure that the maximum size or number of attachments contained in one attached message does not exceed the allowed values. The ID of the individual attachment is given in the AttachmentIdentifier field and is formed from the ID of the attachment message and the SHA1 check calculated from the contents of the attachment. The actual attachment file is given base64-encoded in the AttachmentContent field. The name of the attachment file (name.file extension) is given in the AttachmentName field. If the sender has marked the attachment as confidential, it is indicated in the AttachmentSecurityClass field, so-called With SEI code, e.g. SEI01. The attachment type is given in the AttachmentMimeType field. Allowed values are image/jpeg, image/png and application/pdf. The service provider checks the correctness of the file type upon receipt. For PDF documents, the only type allowed is PDF/A

The WasteHero system enables a single PDF document to be attached to an entire invoice batch (e.g. up to 10,000 invoices) by integrating with external invoicing services in accordance with the FINVOICE guidelines. The ERP system generates the invoice data, including the required metadata and a unique identifier in the FINVOICE message, allowing the invoicing service to correctly associate the attachment with the corresponding invoices.

When an invoice batch is created, users can define a PDF attachment—such as a service update notice—that is to be included with all invoices in the batch. The ERP system includes an **AttachmentDetails** section in the FINVOICE message, containing an **AttachmentIdentifier** that links the attachment to the invoices. The invoicing service then uses this identifier to associate the attachment file with the correct invoice batch.

The ERP system ensures that attachment references are correctly formatted and included in the FINVOICE message, and validates that the correct metadata is sent. This guarantees that attachments remain properly linked to invoices, even when the files themselves are stored externally in the invoicing service.
Clarifications based on customer feedback:
**Invoice data generation:** Ensures that the invoicing service can correctly associate the attachment with the invoice batch.

**Attachment reference in the FINVOICE message:** The ERP system includes the required AttachmentDetails in the FINVOICE message, indicating that an attachment exists and providing the AttachmentIdentifier for correct linking.
**Correct attachment linking:** The ERP system ensures that the correct attachments are linked to the correct invoices by sending structured metadata to the invoicing service.
**Integration with external invoicing services:** The ERP system integrates seamlessly with external invoicing systems to ensure smooth attachment handling and compliance with FINVOICE standards.
**Batch-level attachments:** Users can attach a single document (e.g. a service update notice) to an entire invoice batch, eliminating the need to manually attach it to each individual invoice.

**Attachment metadata in FINVOICE:** The ERP system includes the AttachmentDetails section in the FINVOICE message, ensuring that attachments are correctly linked to invoices in the external invoicing system.
**Automatic validation:** The system validates attachment metadata before submission to prevent missing or incompatible attachments.
**Audit and compliance support:** The system logs all invoice–attachment associations, ensuring traceability and regulatory compliance.
**Error handling:** If the attachment identifier does not match a file in the invoicing service, the system flags the issue before submission so the user can correct it.
A waste management company generates a batch of 5,000 invoices for monthly waste collection services. A service update notice must be attached to all invoices.
1. The invoicing user uploads the PDF file to the external invoicing service, where it is assigned a unique identifier.
2. The ERP system generates the invoice data and includes this identifier in the FINVOICE message under AttachmentDetails.
3. The invoicing service uses the identifier to automatically link the document to all invoices in the batch.
4. When customers view their invoices in their online banking portal, they can see both the invoice and the attached service update document.
If necessary, a pdf attachment must be attached to the entire batch of invoices (e.g. 10,000 invoices).

If an attachment is added to the invoice, the attachment file can be ready at the invoicing broker's service. The FINVOICE message contains the information that there will be an ATTACHMENT to the invoice and the identification information used to identify the attachment. The self-added attachment is ready in the invoicing service and is not transmitted in FINVOICE format.
To enable the ability to review invoice attachments during the verification process, the external invoicing system must meet the following integration requirements:
1. **Attachment retrieval API**
1. The external system must provide an API endpoint through which WasteHero ERP can request and retrieve invoice attachments.
2. The API must support parameters such as invoice number, customer identifier, and attachment type in order to retrieve specific files.
2. **Metadata linking to attachment files**
1. Each attachment must be stored in the external system with metadata that links it to the correct invoice.
2. The required metadata fields must include the following:
1. Invoice number (to ensure an exact match of the attachment).
2. Attachment identifier (as defined in the FINVOICE AttachmentDetails section).
3. File type (e.g. PDF/A, JPEG, PNG).
4. Attachment name and description.
3. **Storage and access control**
1. The external system must securely store invoice attachments and allow authorised ERP users to access and retrieve them.
2. Role-based access control must be implemented to comply with security policies.
4. **Real-time or on-demand retrieval**
1. The external invoicing system should support real-time retrieval of attachments when a user selects an invoice for review in WasteHero ERP.
2. WasteHero must be able to request attachments dynamically via the API rather than relying on preloaded storage.
5. **Standardised attachment formats**
1. Attachment files should be available in standardised formats to ensure compatibility.
2. Supported formats include PDF/A, JPEG, and PNG, and the metadata must specify the file type and file size.
6. **Error handling and response mechanism**
1. If an attachment is missing or cannot be retrieved, the external system must return a structured error response.
2. WasteHero ERP must be able to display an appropriate error message to inform users of missing or inaccessible attachments.

By ensuring that these integration requirements are met, WasteHero can enable invoice review together with attachments while maintaining a streamlined process between the ERP system and external invoicing systems. Please let us know if further clarifications are needed to meet organisational expectations.

The WasteHero ERP system enables users to review invoices and their attachments during the invoice verification process. This functionality ensures that users can inspect both the invoice content and the attachments exactly as they were sent to the customer. Attachments are displayed in their intended format, providing full visibility for quality control, auditing, and customer support purposes. Key functionalities include:

**Integrated invoice and attachment viewing:** When a user opens a sent invoice for review in the ERP system, the invoice and its related attachments are retrieved and displayed in the correct format.
**Metadata-based attachment linking:** Attachments are linked using metadata defined in the FINVOICE AttachmentDetails section, ensuring accurate retrieval.

**Real-time data retrieval:** If attachments are stored externally (e.g. in an invoicing or archiving system), the ERP system retrieves the attachment dynamically and displays it alongside the invoice.
**User-friendly review interface:** Users can browse invoice pages, navigate between attachments, and view attachment details such as name, type, and confidentiality status.
**Audit and compliance support:** The system logs all invoice and attachment data, supporting internal audits and compliance with invoicing regulations.
**Error detection and resolution:** Users can verify the existence and correctness of attachments and ensure corrective actions are taken if discrepancies are identified.

A waste management company’s invoicing specialist reviews a sent invoice to ensure that all required documents are included. The invoice relates to waste treatment services and should include an attachment showing updated tariffs.

The invoicing specialist selects the invoice in the ERP system and opens it for review. The system retrieves the invoice and its attachment from the external invoicing service. The attachment appears as a separate page following the main invoice content, clearly displaying the updated tariff information as expected.

Once the invoicing specialist is satisfied that both the invoice and the attachment match what was sent to the customer, they close the review view. This verification process ensures that all invoicing documents meet customer expectations and regulatory requirements, reducing the likelihood of disputes or errors.
When the sent invoice is opened for review, the attachments sent with the invoice are also seen.

With the WasteHero system, users can add custom text to invoices either on a per-customer basis or across multiple invoices at once. The text is displayed directly on the invoice and is also included in the FINVOICE material exported to external invoicing systems.
**Custom text input:** Users can enter individual messages for single invoices or apply the same message to a batch of invoices in one step.
**Free-text field:** A dedicated section on the invoice allows additional text to be displayed.
**Bulk application:** The system supports mass application of messages to selected invoices.
**Export to FINVOICE format:** All added text is included in the FINVOICE data and transferred to the external invoicing system.
**Audit and accounting:** All added text is stored as part of the invoicing history.
**Seamless template integration:** The custom text field integrates with invoice templates.

The WasteHero system supports the automatic creation of invoices either as **gross** or **net** invoices, in accordance with company policies and customer-specific settings. Gross invoices display amounts inclusive of VAT, while net invoices display amounts exclusive of VAT.
Information on whether a customer is invoiced using a gross or net invoice is stored in the customer profile in WasteHero and is automatically applied when invoices are generated.
**Default gross/net settings:** Ensures consistency and reduces manual adjustments.
**Customer-specific overrides:** Authorized users can override the default setting at the customer level.
**Automatic VAT calculation:** The system dynamically calculates and displays VAT based on the selected invoice type.
**Dynamic invoice formatting:** Invoice templates automatically adapt to display either net or gross amounts.
**Data transfer to external invoicing systems:** The gross/net setting is exported as part of the invoice metadata.
**Access control:** Only authorized personnel can modify the gross/net setting.

The WasteHero system supports **reverse charge VAT invoicing**, enabling the waste companies to issue invoices where the VAT liability is transferred to the buyer for applicable goods and services, such as the sale of scrap metal and the handling of construction waste.
**Automatic application of reverse charge VAT:** The system automatically applies reverse charge VAT when a product or service marked as subject to reverse VAT is added to an invoice.
**Support for mixed VAT scenarios:** A single invoice can include both standard VAT and reverse charge VAT transactions.
**Invoice compliance requirements:** The invoice includes required legal references, the buyer's VAT number, and a clear indication that reverse charge VAT applies.
**Integration with VAT reporting:** Reverse charge VAT transactions are included in sales reports as required.
**Data transfer to external invoicing systems:** Reverse charge VAT information is exported as part of the invoice metadata in FINVOICE format.
**Auditability and traceability:** The system records all invoices containing reverse charge VAT.
An event must be created in the application such that invoicing data can be generated from the same event. The following is the schema of an event (not all events will require these details):
Date
Product/service
Product name / Service name
Waste price
Transport price
Quantity (pcs, m³)
Weight (t/kg)
VAT price 0%
VAT price 24%
Total price with 0% VAT
Total price with 24% VAT
Registration number
Accounts
Cost centers
Free-text comment field
Customer number
Contractor
Load unloading location
Event identifier
Service responsibility
Origin
Direction
Municipality
Price according to the shared collection point's group percentage
Event comments
**Eco-fee** (also a price formation component that must be visible on the event)

The WasteHero system will store event-specific data as part of the invoicing data for events. Events must include all the information required to generate an invoice, including the VAT rates tied to the event date. The application must also be able to generate events that are not intended to be invoiced.
During the implementation phase, the data that drives what information is generated for an event will be defined.
For each event, only the information relevant to that event type is generated.
Event data has to be included in the FINVOICE material in accordance with the FINVOICE specification.
The same invoice can include multiple VAT rates and cost centres.
Event data can also be generated via a weighbridge integration or a cashier/POS system integration.

When generating invoicing data, the WasteHero application extracts the invoice delivery–related information from the data stored in the customer profiles, in accordance with the waste management companies’ requirements.
The mandatory data required by the system includes
customer identifiers,
the invoice delivery method, and
the billing address.

Waste management companies can define additional mandatory fields to meet their own needs. A customer identifier refers to a CustomerID, which is a 6–9 digit numeric sequence. The sequence is running/serial and uniquely identifies the customer record.
The application:
records how the invoice was sent (e.g., as an e-invoice, by email, or on paper)

ensures that the required customer details (e.g., Business ID, e-invoice address) exist before generating an invoice. If critical information is missing, the system must prevent the finalisation of an invoice until the missing data has been added.
notifies invoicing users of invalid data and lists the issues for the users to correct the data before continuing invoicing.
Automatic extraction of customer data from customer records
The invoice data file must comply with the FINVOICE 3.0 standard
Validation and error prevention
Transfer of invoicing data to external invoicing systems
The waste management companies are responsible for defining the information that guides invoicing data generation.
The basic FINVOICE data is defined as common for all companies, and if deviations are needed, this is possible company-specifically as a **change request**.
The status of invoices created from events must be visible at the event level.
The default status categories for events in the WasteHero system are:
**In progress:** events that have been recorded, but for which an invoice has not yet been created.
**Sent:** events for which an invoice and invoice data have been generated, but the invoice data has not yet been sent to the invoicing system.
**Error:** events that contain incorrect data or where errors occurred during the transfer.
**Completed:** an invoice has been created from the event and the invoice data has been successfully sent to and confirmed by the invoicing system.

For example: events transferred to invoicing are updated to the status **Sent**. An invoice in **Sent** status can still be updated if necessary. The invoice data can then be corrected and re-sent. Once the transfer is successful, the data can be updated to status **Completed**. The naming of statuses can also be implemented differently, as long as the functionality remains the same.
Events have process-based statuses that update automatically as the workflow progresses.
Event statuses are visible both in a summary view and in customer-specific information.
Events can be edited until they are marked as completed and the invoice has been successfully transferred to the invoicing system.
Incorrect event data can be corrected after an invoicing run if the invoicing data has not yet been received by the external system.
Here is the English translation:

Financial data is defined through a structured pricing and cost framework in which price lists, cost centers, VAT classifications, and accounting accounts are predefined at the product and service level. This ensures that the correct pricing and financial data are automatically applied when services are used, minimizing manual input and reducing errors.

The system allows office users to manage and adjust financial configurations such as cost allocations, service pricing, and accounting accounts. All relevant financial data flows consistently from event creation through invoicing, accounting, and reporting. This ensures that waste management companies can handle large product catalogs with varying pricing structures while maintaining financial accuracy and compliance with municipal tariffs and national regulations.
1. Background data export**
The system processes a wide range of background data for each event, including:
**Price lists:** The system assigns a predefined price list to each event or service, ensuring consistent pricing based on established tariffs and contracts.
**Cost centers:** Cost centers can be defined and linked to each product, service, or event, reflecting cost allocation for budgeting and financial analysis.

**Accounting accounts:** Events are linked to the appropriate accounts for financial reporting. For example, transport revenue, waste treatment fees, and VAT liabilities are posted to their respective accounts.
**VAT rates:** The system supports multiple VAT rates, including rates that vary based on the event date, ensuring compliance with national regulations.
**Other metadata:** Custom fields can store additional financial data required by the organization for accurate invoicing and reporting.
2. Configurable financial data management**
The system allows office users to manually add, update, and maintain cost centers, VAT rates, and accounting accounts. This includes:
Assigning accounts to different services or products during product creation.
Dynamically linking financial data to transactions based on tariff rules (e.g. primary and secondary service responsibility pricing).
Modifying product financial settings when tariffs or regulations change annually.
3. Integration for reporting**
Exported data integrates with external accounting and business intelligence (BI) systems to provide real-time visibility and detailed financial reporting. This integration supports:
Automatic synchronization of cost centers, revenue, and VAT between the ERP and external systems.
Generation of reports detailing revenue, costs, and VAT breakdowns for audits and statutory inspections.
4. Data flow consistency**

The system ensures that financial data flows through all stages of the invoicing process, from event creation to final reporting. Data consistency prevents discrepancies between invoicing and accounting records, thereby minimizing errors.
5. Scalability for large product catalogs**

Due to the complexity of waste management operations, the system supports hundreds of products, each with unique pricing and accounting requirements. Structured export capabilities enable companies to maintain accurate data across diverse services and customer types.
A waste management company creates an invoice for mixed waste collected in the municipality of Tampere. The system processes the event data as follows:
Product: Mixed waste (240L waste container)
Treatment fee: €3.49
Transport fee: €2.83
Total price (net): €6.32
Total price (gross): €7.93
1. Cost center: 20/200/3 (responsibility for an individual waste collection service)
2. Accounting:
Account 30002: Waste treatment revenue (€3.49)
Account 30005: Transport revenue (€2.83)
Account 2939: VAT payable (€1.61)

The system exports this data to the company’s accounting software, ensuring compliance with reporting standards. With this visibility, office users can confidently manage price lists and financial data, knowing that each event is correctly classified for both invoicing and financial reporting purposes.

With regard to all events, services, etc., it is possible to export cost centers, accounts and other necessary background information behind the euros in the system, so that the events flow correctly for invoicing, accounting and reporting.

The WasteHero system assigns products and services to the correct accounts based on rules defined during the waste management company’s implementation. Based on these rules, the system can direct charges for the same product or service in the invoicing data to different accounts and/or cost centers as part of the FINVOICE data.

The cost center information for an event may consist of multiple components and data points. These may include, for example, the product’s cost center, the reception point’s cost center, and the service responsibility information defined in the contract.
The structure and content of cost centers are defined during implementation, and for any change needs, WasteHero support can assist in modifying the formats.

Accounts for different products are defined in connection with product price lists. For example, VAT may be posted to a VAT account, waste treatment fees to their own account, and transport fees to a separate account.
When entering account information, automatic completion is used: the user can start typing an account, and the system will suggest matching account options.

Accounts are managed in the settings, where properties such as account number, name, and validity period can be defined. All accounts in use within the system are visible in the settings. Account management can be performed by the waste management company’s main users.
Account information can be set for each individual pricing component of a product.
Cost centers are determined for events according to configuration rules.
Cost center and accounting account information is exported as part of the FINVOICE data.
Accounting accounts can be centrally managed within the system.
Define the desired cost center logic.
Invoicing system
Cash register system
Weighing system

The same product must be able to be routed to a different account and cost center based on e.g. price list or region. The cost location information must be forwarded as a correctly defined part of the FINVOICE material.

An invoicing surcharge must be able to be added to an invoice depending on the invoicing method, e.g. a paper invoice surcharge, email invoice surcharge, direct payment surcharge. The invoicing-method surcharge must be generated automatically for customers to whom the given invoicing method applies. However, it must be possible to globally toggle the invoicing-method surcharge on and off.

The invoicing-method surcharges can be different in amount for different customers, such as business customers and consumer customers. In the WasteHero system, an invoicing surcharge can by default be linked to paper invoices, direct payments, and email invoices.

The invoicing surcharge function works as follows: a separate product is created for the invoicing surcharge (e.g. paper invoice surcharge). The surcharge is shown automatically whenever the customer is to receive a paper invoice. The invoicing user can switch it off if necessary. It is possible to define the value of the invoicing surcharge according to the customer type.
The following situation-specific exceptions can be applied:
Removing the surcharge from the first invoice.
Preventing the application of surcharges when they are not included in the tariff.
Allowing different surcharges for business and consumer customers.
Manually removing individual surcharges from individual invoices.
Defining different surcharge amounts for business and consumer customers.
Waste companies can define cases in which the system does not apply surcharges.
Surcharges can vary based on customer types.
Users at waste companies can manually remove surcharges from individual invoices.
The surcharge amount is visible on the invoice sent to the customer.
The following can be used as selection criteria for invoicing events per invoicing run, for example:
Municipality
A monetary threshold for the total amount (€), below which invoices are not generated
Invoicing period, i.e. customer-specific invoicing frequency (e.g. preferred every 3 months vs every 1 month)
Time period
Customer type (e.g. weighbridge customer, regional collection customer)
Events of a certain type of service (e.g. sludge events)
Reception location (if events from a specific reception facility for weighbridge customers should be invoiced separately)
Service responsibility for the invoicing events
Different selection criteria must be definable for invoice printing. It must be possible to invoice only a subset of the events linked to a customer.
Customisable invoice printing rules:**
The user sets the conditions according to which events are excluded.
Supported filters:**
**Municipality:** include/exclude events from specific municipalities.
**Minimum invoice amount:** monetary threshold (e.g. invoices under €10 are carried over to the next invoicing period and accumulated with future events until the threshold is reached).
**Invoicing frequency:** customer-specific invoicing periods (e.g. monthly, quarterly, yearly).
**Time period:** events based on date.
**Customer type:** invoicing restrictions applied to specific customer groups.
**Events of a specific contract/service type:** restrict invoicing to events related to certain services (e.g. sludge collection events).
**Reception location:** separate invoicing based on waste reception facilities for certain customer segments.
**Service responsibility:** ensure that only those events are invoiced which meet the defined service responsibility conditions.
In addition, other fields can also be configured as criteria for invoicing selection.
The fields to be visible as selection criteria in the invoice printing view need to be defined.
The system allows users to define invoicing rules at both the service and customer levels and to specify which events are invoiced separately and which are grouped together. The key settings include:
**Billing interval:** Immediate invoicing for certain events (e.g. urgent or one-off services) and scheduled invoicing for recurring services.
**Service types:** Different service categories, such as waste collection, septic tank emptying, or other utility services, can have separate invoicing criteria.
**Customer preferences:** Customized invoicing options based on customer contracts, ensuring that tailored, service-type-specific invoices are generated.

**Invoice grouping:** The system should also prevent conflicting groupings. For example, a one-off service such as septic tank emptying should not be delayed by other recurring services in the invoicing process. In contrast, mixed waste services can be bundled into a semi-annual invoice based on predefined customer rules.

From an operational perspective, this configuration improves cash flow management, minimizes invoicing errors, and provides customers with clear and timely invoices for different services. Users can easily define and manage these rules through the ERP user interface, ensuring that the applied invoicing terms are fully traceable.

The invoicing system also integrates with external financial management tools to ensure that separate invoices are processed correctly and comply with industry billing standards. Reporting enables finance teams to track both immediate and periodic invoices, helping to ensure compliance and financial accuracy.
A waste management company serves a customer who owns both a residential property and a summer cottage. The customer has different invoicing settings for each service:
Mixed waste collection is invoiced twice a year (semi-annually).
Septic tank emptying services are invoiced immediately after each service.
In the ERP system, an office user defines separate invoicing rules for the customer:
Mixed waste collection events are grouped under a semi-annual billing schedule confirmed every six months.
Septic tank emptying events are set for immediate invoicing.

In September, the company empties the septic tank at the customer’s summer cottage. The system recognizes the event as requiring immediate invoicing and generates an invoice during the same week. Later, in December, the system generates a semi-annual invoice for mixed waste services, bundling all related events from the second half of the year. This ensures that both services are invoiced accurately in accordance with the customer’s service agreements.

Each service or event type is linked to a predefined billing cycle, such as monthly, quarterly, or annually. The system automatically sorts and processes events according to their assigned schedules, preventing conflicts between different billing cycles. Users can define and adjust billing intervals through an intuitive user interface, ensuring that changes in customer preferences or contracts are reflected without affecting other services.

During the billing process, events are grouped according to their billing period. The system validates events before invoices are generated, ensuring that only relevant events are included. This minimizes manual work and improves accuracy.
**Automatic billing cycle assignment:** Services or events are assigned specific billing intervals (e.g. monthly, quarterly, annually).
**Independent service-based billing:** Different services under the same customer account can follow individual billing schedules.
**Automatic event grouping:** Events are automatically sorted and billed according to their assigned schedules.
**Flexible user configuration:** Office users can modify service- or property-specific billing cycles without impacting other billing settings.
**Invoice preview and confirmation:** Users can review upcoming invoices to ensure correctness before finalization.
**Seamless integration:** Billing schedules are synchronized with external financial and accounting systems to ensure consistency.
A customer owns both a residential property and a summer cottage. The customer wants:
Quarterly billing for regular mixed waste collection at the residential property.
Annual billing for services related to the summer cottage.
In the WasteHero ERP system, an office user sets individual billing cycles for each property:
1. Mixed waste collection events for the residential property are billed every three months.
2. Waste management services for the summer cottage are assigned an annual billing cycle.

Both properties generate multiple billable events throughout the year. In December, the ERP system consolidates all events related to the summer cottage into a single annual invoice. At the same time, quarterly invoices continue to be generated for the residential property, ensuring that the customer’s preferences are followed precisely.

The WasteHero system provides a flexible invoicing structure that allows companies to define how events are grouped on invoices. This ensures that customers receive invoices formatted according to their preferences, with relevant events either combined into single line items or separated by type. The system supports customer-specific bundling, enabling waste management companies to tailor invoice layouts based on contracts or operational requirements.

When creating invoice data, users can define grouping rules based on product types, services, or other predefined criteria. The system then applies these settings during invoice generation and ensures that similar events—such as storage location emptying, base charges, and pallet deliveries—are handled either as separate or bundled line items.

The bundling logic is managed through customer-specific settings, allowing each company to define which event types are combined and which are listed separately. This prevents invoices from containing an excessive number of line items, reducing complexity for both customers and finance teams. The system also ensures that changes to invoice grouping do not affect the underlying event data, preserving the accuracy of financial reporting and accounting.
**Customer-specific bundling rules:**
Users can define event bundling settings per customer, specifying which events are combined into a single line item and which remain separate.
**Flexible grouping options:**
Events can be grouped by product category, service type, or predefined rules, such as:
All storage location emptying events shown as one consolidated line item
Base service fees shown as a separate line item
Pallet deliveries listed as individual events
**Automatic application in invoicing:**
Once defined, bundling rules are automatically applied when invoices are generated, reducing manual work and ensuring consistency.
**Customization according to business requirements:**
Each waste management company can define how events should be structured, ensuring alignment with internal practices and customer preferences.
**Integration with financial reporting:**
Bundled events remain separate in financial records while appearing grouped on invoices, ensuring reporting accuracy without compromising data integrity.
**Audit and compliance support:**
The system logs all bundling configurations and changes made, ensuring transparency and audit readiness.
A waste management company creates an invoice for a commercial customer that receives multiple services. The company defines the customer’s invoice structure as follows:
Storage location emptying events (weekly collections) are bundled into a single invoice line titled **“Waste container collection – 12 collections.”**
Base service fees are shown as a separate line titled **“Monthly service fee.”**
Pallet deliveries are listed as individual line items, each detailing the type and weight of collected waste.

When the invoice is generated, the system automatically applies these grouping settings, ensuring that the customer receives a clear and well-structured invoice. This minimises confusion, improves payment processing, and meets customer expectations.

The WasteHero application enables the configuration of recurring fees based on predefined schedules and customer contracts. Once these fees have been configured, they are automatically recognised as billable events, ensuring that all relevant charges are generated correctly and on time. In addition, the system supports variable pricing models, meaning that seasonal fees can be updated annually without manual adjustments for each billing period.

**Automatic identification of time-based fees:** The system recognises and processes non event-based billing items and ensures that scheduled fees (e.g. annual property charges) are included on invoices.
**Configurable billing rules:** Users can define billing frequencies (monthly, quarterly, annually) and specify which products or services are eligible for automatic billing.
**Flexible fee management:** Seasonal fees can be adjusted dynamically based on property type, contracts, or regulatory updates.

**Customer-specific fee configuration:** The system allows different types of properties or customer classes to have separate billing rules. For example, permanent residences and holiday homes can have different annual fees.
**Seamless integration with invoicing workflows:** These fees are processed in the same way as other billable events, ensuring consistency in financial reporting and customer account management.

A waste management company charges an annual base fee for all properties within its service area. The company configures the following billing rules in the WasteHero system:
Permanent residences: Annual fee of €150, invoiced in January.
Holiday homes: Annual fee of €75, invoiced in April.

Project-based invoicing allows the user to link multiple projects to a single customer. The user can create detailed, site-specific invoices that comply with the customer's requirements and the structures of financial reporting.
Projects (or work sites) can be created in three ways:
As separate properties linked to the customer
As separate contracts for a property or a customer
As a customer hierarchy, where projects are customers linked to a main customer, each with its own contracts

For weighbridge customers, separate work sites can be set up as separate contracts for the customer, and a work site field can be added to the contract. If desired, the WasteHero application can be configured so that different projects are invoiced on separate invoices and that each of them can have its own references.

Customer-specific weighbridge integrations

The system ensures that the minimum charge complies with the contract dates, preventing overbilling in cases where customer contracts start or end mid-season. This logic is particularly relevant for services such as seasonal waste collection, where some customers may have few or no events but are still subject to an annual minimum fee in accordance with municipal waste management regulations.

**Automatic minimum fee calculation:** The system calculates the total amount of the invoice for the billing period and checks whether it meets the minimum charge requirement. If the final invoice amount is below the defined minimum, the system adds a surcharge to cover the difference.
**Tariff-based minimum amounts:** The system reads the minimum charge levels from the tariffs, ensuring that the correct amount is applied automatically.

**Customer contract validation:** The system checks the contract period before applying the minimum charge. If the customer’s contract starts or ends mid-season, the system ensures that the minimum charge is not incorrectly applied to the new owner.
**Case-specific application:** The minimum charge feature is configurable, allowing companies to enable or disable it according to business needs.
**Seamless integration with invoicing:** Minimum charge adjustments are automatically included in the invoicing material.

The WasteHero system supports both private-law and public-law invoicing, ensuring that invoices comply with applicable legislation. The system automatically determines the correct classification based on predefined criteria such as customer type, product or service classification, or regional regulations. This information is then included in the invoicing material and transferred via FINVOICE to external invoicing and collection systems.
The system provides two primary ways to handle public-law and private-law invoicing:
The system can automatically distinguish between public-law transactions (e.g. municipal waste collection) and private-law transactions (e.g. commercial waste services).
When an invoice is created, the system applies statutory classification rules and determines whether transactions should be invoiced separately.
This ensures that the correct legal collection processes, VAT application, and enforcement mechanisms are applied under the appropriate legal framework.
The system supports the creation of a single invoice that includes both public-law and private-law transactions.
Each invoice line includes a legally compliant classification identifier indicating whether the transaction falls under public law or private law.
When exported via FINVOICE, the system attaches metadata indicating which transactions fall under public law or private law.

requirements.summary: 3.4.29 Public and private law sales – billing
acceptance_criteria: The WasteHero system enables waste management companies to handle public-law and private-law sales transactions flexibly, allowing them to be invoiced either together on the same invoice or separately, according to user preferences. Because public and private transactions follow different collection and enforcement processes, the system ensures that each transaction is correctly classified and that this classification is transferred at the invoice line level in the FINVOICE data.
Users can configure the system as follows:
Combine public and private sales transactions on a single invoice while maintaining a clear identifier at the invoice line level.
Separate public and private sales into different invoices, ensuring that each follows the correct collection process.
Customize invoice processing rules at the company level, taking into account that waste management companies may have different billing structures and internal practices.
The system ensures that public-law and private-law transactions are distinguished using metadata, enabling correct enforcement, VAT application, and financial reporting.

**User-defined invoice grouping:** Users can choose whether public and private sales transactions are invoiced on the same invoice or on separate invoices. This configuration can be defined at the customer level or by transaction type.
**Legal classification at the line level:** Each invoice line includes a classification indicating whether the charge falls under public law or private law.
**Automatic compliance with legislation in collection processes:** The system identifies statutory, tariff-based public services and distinguishes them from commercial/private sales.
**Integration with FINVOICE and external systems:** The system automatically exports the public/private classification as part of the FINVOICE data.
**Customizable enforcement handling:** Waste management companies can define whether invoices are handled through separate enforcement authorities or general enforcement processes.

The application enables users to manually enter invoicing events for services that do not originate from automated integrations, such as weighbridge systems or route management. Users can select the appropriate product from the catalogue, enter the invoicing details, and create an invoice while ensuring that the related cost centres, ledger accounts, and VAT rates are applied correctly.

Manual invoicing is fully integrated into the WasteHero application, allowing waste management companies to process a wide range of events outside their core services while maintaining structured financial administration.
**Manual entry of invoicing events:** Users can create invoices manually by selecting the appropriate product in the system, ensuring that financial classifications remain consistent.

**Product-based invoicing:** Even when an event is created manually, it is linked to an existing product (e.g., “land rent” or “expert work”), ensuring that financial allocations are applied correctly.
**Predefined financial allocations:** The system ensures that manual invoicing events follow predefined accounting rules and automatically assigns cost centres, VAT percentages, and revenue accounts.

**Support for non-core business events:** Enables waste management companies to invoice services outside waste management, such as equipment rental, land rent, water consumption, or consulting services.
**Customisable invoice lines:** Users can enter event-specific details while still following predefined pricing structures, enabling flexibility while maintaining structured invoicing.
**Audit and reporting integration:** Manual invoices are logged and reported in the same way as automated events, ensuring financial traceability and audit compliance.
A waste management company needs to invoice customers for services that are not generated through automated integrations. Two typical cases are:
1. **Land rent**
A customer rents land from the waste company, but the fee does not arise through an automated process.
The invoicing user creates an event manually and selects “Land rent” from the predefined product catalogue.
The system assigns the correct revenue account and VAT rate based on the product configuration.
The invoice is created and transferred to the external invoicing system, ensuring financial compliance.
2. **Expert work invoice**
A customer requests consulting services from the waste company.
The service is not recorded through time tracking or operational systems; instead, the waste company has an “Expert work” product with an hourly rate.
The invoicing user manually selects “Expert work,” enters the number of billable hours, and creates the invoice.
The system ensures correct allocation of VAT and revenue, avoiding financial inconsistencies.
Users can manually add an e-invoicing address to the customer information.
Users can override or modify e-invoicing addresses and at the same time prevent future integration updates from automatically replacing them.
E-invoicing addresses are also retrieved automatically via integration, without requiring user approval. The e-invoicing integration is handled in requirement 3.11.24.
E-invoicing addresses can be edited and added manually.

A setting can be added to an e-invoicing address indicating that the recorded e-invoicing address must not be overwritten via integration. This is implemented using a field where a checkbox indicates that this field must not be overwritten by the integration.

It must also be possible to edit e-invoicing addresses manually, and it must be possible to add new e-invoicing addresses, even if the information is not available from the integration. The system must also handle a situation where the manual information is correct and the integration would always result in incorrect information.

The ERP system acts as the master system for invoicing data, and all changes to the billing address (postal address, email address, or e-invoicing company address) made in the ERP system are synchronised with the invoicing system.

When a customer’s billing address changes, the ERP system updates the correct billing address via the integration interface. Once the new billing address has been updated, it is applied to all open invoices and payment reminders for that customer in the invoicing system.
Payment reminders must be sent to the updated address after a change, for example when a property management company changes.

If a customer had property manager A until the end of January and from the beginning of February has property manager B, reminders and other open invoices must be sent to the new property manager B. This ensures that invoices and reminders are delivered to the correct address even if the property manager has changed.
In the ERP system, the new billing address is updated and then transferred to the invoice operator via the integration interface.
Billing address details are updated in the invoicing system whenever they change
New billing details are applied to open invoices
Invoicing system
When the billing address changes, it must be possible to update the new information via the interface to any open invoices that have already been transferred to the invoice broker.
Invoice payment reminders must go to the updated address after the change, for example when the hosting agency changes.

requirements.summary: 3.4.33 Shared service events on the invoice
acceptance_criteria: Each subscriber in a shared service is assigned an individual invoice line, with the amount determined according to their percentage share. The payment share for each subscriber can range from 0–100%, with the total across all subscribers required to equal exactly 100% — neither more nor less. When an invoicing user manually defines the percentage shares within an agreement, the application issues a warning if the total exceeds or falls below 100%. It is also possible to configure whether a participant with a 0% share should appear on the invoice or be excluded from it.

By default, the application should divide the invoiceable sum equally among the subscribers to a shared service but can also allow for the total sum to be split based on user-defined agreements. Each exported invoice entry includes information about the shared service, the customer’s percentage share, and the total service cost.

This functionality is implemented by defining the shared service as a **property group** within the system. The property group includes the relevant properties, and the desired service is shared among them. Usage shares for the service are then defined individually for each participant.
The application divides the costs of a shared service among the subscribers to that service  according to  pre-defined percentage shares.
Each subscriber receives their own invoice line based on their payment share.
The sum of all percentage shares must equal exactly 100%, and the application must return an error message if the total is not exactly 100 %.
By default, the total cost is to be split equally amongst the subscribers, but invoicing users can configure alternative distribution rules per agreement.
The invoicing data includes the shared service, the customer’s percentage share, and the total cost of the service allocated to that customer.
Shared services are created as **property groups**, to which properties are added and share percentages are defined for each participant.

acceptance_criteria: A new participant can also be added to a shared service retroactively, in which case all events falling within the specified date range are redistributed based on the updated participation data.
Shared service events are dynamically distributed based on the participant allocation.
New events may be generated, for example, when a new participant is added to the shared service retroactively.
Participants can be linked to a shared service retroactively as members of the shared service.
